## Decodable Reader — "At the Park"
Publisher: Laprea Publishing (StructuredLiteracy.com)
Level: GREEN set — R-Controlled Vowels

Skills in scope:
- ar (x2)
- or, ore (x2)
- ar, or, ore review (x2)
- er (x2)
- ir, ur (x2)
- er, ir, ur; w+or (x2)
- R-controlled vowels review (x2)

Warm-up words assigned:
before, birds, chirping, disturb, first, market, morning, observe, park, perch, turtle, worms

Irregular high-frequency words assigned:
are, do, father, from, of, said, see, should, there, two, want, water

Full text of the book:
Page 4: "It is the first morning of summer. Carl's father said he could spend time at the park with his friend, Peter."
Page 5: "The park is the perfect place for Carl and Peter to go. There is so much for them to do and observe."
Page 6: "They get to the park first thing in the morning. Carl and Peter do not want to disturb the birds."
Page 7: "The two friends perch on a bench. Carl says, 'The birds are chirping a lot!'"
Page 8: "Next, Carl and Peter go visit the park's turtle. His name is Marvin. Marvin loves the worms from Carl and Peter."
Page 9: "The next stop is the water. The two pals observe the ducks swirling in the pond."
Page 10: "After the visit with the ducks, Carl and Peter go over to the slides and swings. Peter slides thirty times, and Carl whirls and twirls on the swings."
Page 11: "The pals are quite thirsty, so they get a drink from the market. Peter gets a purple drink, and Carl just gets water."
Page 12: "They stop to see the otters before they go. Carl and Peter love to observe them! Peter is an otter expert!"
Page 13: "It is time for the partners to return home. Carl and Peter had a perfect time at the park!"
